title: The Rank Ladder — Shadow Multiplicity and Higher Ranks

analysis.object: T856: The Rank Ladder — Shadow Multiplicity and Higher Ranks depth analysis

rank0_shadow.description: Rank 0: L(E,1) != 0. Shadow = EML-2 (simple L-value). Single shadow.

rank1_shadow.description: Rank 1: L(E,1) = 0, L'(E,1) != 0. Shadow = EML-3 (derivative). Single EML-3 shadow.

rank2_shadow.description: Rank 2: L(E,1) = L'(E,1) = 0, L''(E,1) != 0. Double EML-3 shadow?

rank_r_shadow.description: Rank r: r-th derivative nonzero. Shadow = EML-3 with multiplicity r.

multiplicity_bound.description: Is multiplicity bounded? Each EML-3 oscillation = one derivative zero. Bounded multiplicity = bounded rank.

selmer_controls_multiplicity.description: Selmer group dimension controls multiplicity: dim Sel = rank + dim Sha. Shadow multiplicity = rank.

t856_theorem.description: T856: Rank r corresponds to EML-3 shadow with multiplicity r. Selmer group dimension bounds rank + dim Sha. T852 (Sha finite) + Selmer finite-dim -> rank is finite for all E. T856.

analysis.theorem: T856: The Rank Ladder — Shadow Multiplicity and Higher Ranks (S1136).

key_theorem: T856: The Rank Ladder — Shadow Multiplicity and Higher Ranks (S1136).
